unit.name: CS231n content roadmap: tasks, models, and beyond 2D recognition
unit.description: Transitions to the second half of the lecture, where the course roadmap is introduced: core tasks, model families, distributed training, and frontier topics including generative models, vision-language models, 3D vision, and embodied intelligence.
unit.summary: The course overview organizes CS231n into four themes: deep learning basics, perceiving and understanding the visual world, generative and interactive visual intelligence, and human-centered applications and implications [slides in Part 2 repeatedly show this four-part roadmap]. The lecture first frames image classification as a core task and uses it to motivate linear classifiers, regularization and optimization, and neural networks [slides show a cat example flowing into each of these model ideas]. It then broadens vision beyond image classification to semantic segmentation, object detection, instance segmentation, video classification, multimodal video understanding, and visualization or interpretation [ts=2615s] [ts=2690s]. Next, the lecture introduces models beyond multilayer perceptrons, especially convolutional networks, recurrent networks, transformers, and large-scale distributed training via data parallelism and model parallelism [ts=2700s] [ts=2825s]. The segment then turns to frontier areas beyond 2D recognition: self-supervised learning, generative modeling including diffusion, vision-language models with shared text-image representation spaces, 3D reconstruction and generation, and embodied agents that perceive, plan, and act [ts=3439s] [ts=3596s].

key_points.text: Image classification is introduced as a core computer vision task that anchors the early technical part of the course.
Vision tasks beyond classification include semantic segmentation, object detection, instance segmentation, video classification, and multimodal understanding.
CS231n moves beyond multilayer perceptrons to CNNs, RNNs, and transformers.
Large-scale training uses data parallelism and model parallelism to overcome single-device limits.
Diffusion models reverse a gradual noising process to generate images.
Vision-language models place text and images in a shared representation space for retrieval or generation.
3D vision and embodied intelligence extend vision into reconstruction, grounding, planning, and action.

kp_catalog.name: Modern vision extends beyond classification
kp_catalog.description: The course treats vision as covering segmentation, detection, video, multimodal learning, generative models, vision-language models, 3D vision, and embodied intelligence.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because modern vision extends beyond classification formalizes how the course treats vision as covering segmentation, detection, video, multimodal learning, generative models, vision-language models, 3D vision, and embodied intelligence, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Deep learning resurgence from data, compute, and scale
kp_catalog.description: Why modern neural NLP depends on a set of training and modeling ideas that made deep networks workable after years of difficulty. Large-scale datasets, GPU computation, and neural architectures converged to unlock modern deep learning for vision.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because deep learning resurgence from data, compute, and scale formalizes how why modern neural NLP depends on a set of training and modeling ideas that made deep networks workable after years of difficulty, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.

kp_catalog.name: CS231n curriculum structure and learning objectives
kp_catalog.description: The course is organized around deep learning basics, visual understanding, generative/interactive intelligence, and human-centered AI, with explicit goals for problem formulation, model building, and field awareness.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because cS231n curriculum structure and learning objectives clarifies the course is organized around deep learning basics, visual understanding, generative/interactive intelligence, and human-centered AI, with explicit goals for problem formulation, model building, and field awareness, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
